title: install_from_marketplace skips PluginPolicy entirely — only add_marketplace_*_with_policy variants enforce it
description:
## Severity
Major
## Dimension
security
## Location
src/plugins/manager.rs:532-708, src/plugins/manager.rs:332-372
## Violation
There are explicit `_with_policy` variants for adding marketplaces, but install_from_marketplace and install_from_git both run the actual plugin clone without ever consulting PluginPolicy::blocked_marketplaces or strict_known_marketplaces. Quoted lines:

    pub fn install_from_marketplace(
        &mut self,
        plugin_name: &str,
        marketplace_name: &str,
    ) -> Result<String, PluginError> {
        // Find the marketplace
        let marketplaces = self.list_marketplaces();
        ...
        let commit_sha = match def {
            PluginSourceDef::Url { url, git_ref } => {
                ...
                git_clone(url, &dest, git_ref.as_deref())?

A managed environment that allowlists a single marketplace can still pull in *any* upstream URL named by that marketplace's manifest because the per-plugin source URL is never re-validated against the policy. policy.rs:30-90 explicitly distinguishes user vs managed scope, so this gap silently downgrades managed policies to advisory.
## Mandated refactor
Introduce install_from_marketplace_with_policy and install_from_git_with_policy mirroring the add_marketplace_*_with_policy pattern. Inside the structured-source branch, build a MarketplaceSource (Git / GitHub / Url) from the resolved upstream URL and run policy::check_marketplace_allowed against it before git_clone fires. Make the non-policy installer #[deprecated] for tests-only use, or have it forward to the policy variant with the active managed policy fetched from settings.
## Filed from QA review dispatch
